query: Cambodia H5N1 human fatal cases 2025 affected provinces exposure source epidemiological report

mako.raw:
{"query":"Cambodia H5N1 human fatal cases 2025 affected provinces exposure source epidemiological report","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1143,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://globalbiodefense.com/2025/07/08/cambodia-reports-surge-in-human-infections-with-avian-influenza-ah5n1/","title":"Cambodia Reports Surge in Human Infections with Avian Influenza A(H5N1)","content":"Between January and July 2025, Cambodian health authorities reported 11 laboratory-confirmed cases of human infection with avian influenza A(H5N1), including six fatalities.
Since that re-emergence, Cambodia has confirmed 27 human infections, with a case fatality ratio (CFR) of 44%.
Globally, from 2003 through July 2025, 986 human H5N1 infections have been reported to the World Health Organization (WHO), with 473 deaths—reflecting a high CFR of 48%.
Cambodia’s Ministry of Health, alongside its Ministry of Agriculture and international partners, mobilized national and sub-national rapid response teams to investigate the 2025 outbreak."},{"url":"https://www.cdc.gov/bird-flu/spotlights/global-summary-06262026.html","title":"Global Summary of Human Cases of H5N1 Bird Flu from August 2025 to June 2026 | Bird Flu | CDC","content":"From August 4, 2025 to June 10, 2026, 12 human infections with avian influenza A(H5N1) viruses (H5N1 bird flu) were reported by three countries outside of the United States.
The 12 cases were reported by Bangladesh, Cambodia, and India.
As of June 10, 2026, 1,022 cases have now been reported worldwide since 1997.
Three human bird flu cases with A(H5N1) virus infection (clade 2.3.2.1a) were reported to have occurred in Bangladesh since August 2025.
Cambodia has reported eight human infections with A(H5N1) viruses since CDC's August 2025 summary.
Four of these cases occurred in 2025, and four in 2026."},{"url":"https://afludiary.blogspot.com/2026/04/cambodian-moh-announces-4th-human-h5n1.html","title":"Avian Flu Diary: Cambodian MOH Announces 4th Human H5N1 Case of 2026","content":"Apr 21, 2026 — H5N1 human infection of 2026 … 10 people in 2024, and 18 people in 2025. proved fatal in about 40% of the cases reported by Cambodia since 2023 ...
With thanks for the head's up from @E_A_Karlsson, we have the following announcement from Cambodia's Ministry of Health on their 4th confirmed HPAI H5N1 human infection of 2026 - and 38th in just over 3 years - this time involving a 66 y.o. woman from Svay Rieng province who had recent contact with sick chickens and was confirmed positive on March April 21st.
In February of 2023 an older clade of H5N1 (2.3.2.1.x) reemerged in Cambodia's population after a 9 year absence, spilling over into 6 humans in 2023, 10 people in 2024, and 18 people in 2025.
Unlike the newer clade 2.3.4.4b H5Nx viruses - which have shown much lower mortality rates in the United States - this older clade has proved fatal in about 40% of the cases reported by Cambodia since 2023.
While human-to-human transmission was considered, the available evidence suggests a `shared' exposure instead, with infected poultry the most likely source of infection."},{"url":"https://www.cidrap.umn.edu/avian-influenza-bird-flu/cambodia-reports-2-h5n1-avian-flu-cases-1-fatal","title":"Cambodia reports 2 H5N1 avian flu cases, 1 fatal | CIDRAP","content":"Cambodia reports 2 H5N1 avian flu cases, 1 fatal
The health ministry posted a notice yesterday about the first patient, a 50-year-old man from Svay Rieng province, on its Facebook page.
Today, the ministry posted a notice about a second H5N1 case, which involves a 2-year-old girl from neighboring Prey Veng province who died from her infection, according to a translation posted by AFD.
In February, Cambodia reported a fatal H5N1 case in a 11-year-old girl from Prey Veng province, along with a subsequent infection confirmed in her father, who was asymptomatic and was isolated at a hospital.
The two cases in February marked Cambodia’s first H5N1 infections since 2014."},{"url":"https://beaconbio.org/en/report/?reportid=898b092f-ffec-460b-80ec-8f318a28c0d1&eventid=999496bb-0b95-44ec-85c3-42e5d79b8820","title":"First human case of highly pathogenic avian influenza ...","content":"Feb 15, 2026 — Cambodia reported 30 outbreaks of HPAI (H5N1) in domestic poultry between July 2024 and December 2025. 4143 cases, and 3636 deaths."},{"url":"https://globalriskatlas.com/en/fifth-human-case-of-avian-influenza-h5n1-in-cambodia-in-2026/","title":"Fifth human case of avian influenza (H5N1) in Cambodia ...","date":"6 days ago","content":"6 days ago — July 11, 2026. The fifth human case of highly pathogenic avian influenza (HPAI) (H5N1) in Cambodia in 2026 has been reported.Read more"},{"url":"https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/outbreaks-under-monitoring-in-2026/outbreaks-under-monitoring-week-17-week-ending-26-april-2026","title":"week 17 (week ending 26 April 2026)","content":"Jul 9, 2026 — In 2025, Cambodia reported 18 confirmed avian influenza A(H5N1) human cases, including 9 deaths. Further information, Avian influenza: managing ...Read more"}]}
